2606.06320.title: Learning What to Forget: Improving LLM Unlearning via Learned Token-Level Importance
2606.06320.paper_summary: Machine unlearning aims to remove targeted knowledge from a trained model while preserving its general capabilities. For autoregressive language models, not all tokens in a forget sample are equally relevant to forgetting. Existing approaches either ignore this heterogeneity or rely on auxiliary models, heuristics, or external annotations to estimate each token's relevance for forgetting. We instead characterize it through the interaction with the retain objective: a token is forget-specific to the extent that minimizing the forget loss on that token does not conflict with retain optimality. We formalize this perspective as a joint optimization problem over the model parameters and the token weights and show that, under a natural separation condition, the resulting objective recovers the oracle forget-specific token support. Motivated by this formulation, we introduce Alternating Token-Weighted Unlearning (ATWU), a lightweight framework that jointly learns token forget-specificity and model parameters during unlearning using a simple linear scorer over the hidden states, without external token level supervision. Across TOFU and RWKU, ATWU achieves state of the art forget-retain trade-offs, outperforming sample-level methods, probability-based token weighting heuristics, and auxiliary-model-based approaches. Moreover, the learned scores align substantially better with ground truth forget-specific spans, indicating that ATWU identifies semantically meaningful token level forgetting signals. Overall, our results suggest that retain conflict provides an effective criterion for identifying what language models should forget, enabling unsupervised learning of token level forget-specificity directly from model representations with minimal computational overhead.
2606.06320.paper_summary_zh: 機器遺忘旨在從訓練模型中移除特定知識，同時保留其一般能力。對於自回歸語言模型，遺忘樣本中的所有標記對遺忘的相關性並不相同。現有的方法要麼忽略這種異質性，要麼依賴輔助模型、啟發式方法或外部註釋來估計每個標記對遺忘的相關性。我們則通過與保留目標的互動來表徵這一點：一個標記對遺忘的特異性程度取決於在該標記上最小化遺忘損失是否與保留最佳性相衝突。我們將這一觀點形式化為一個關於模型參數和標記權重的聯合優化問題，並顯示在自然分離條件下，得到的目標恢復了神諭遺忘特定標記的支持。受到這一形式化的啟發，我們引入了交替標記加權遺忘（ATWU），這是一個輕量級框架，通過對隱藏狀態的簡單線性評分器，在遺忘過程中共同學習標記的遺忘特異性和模型參數，而無需外部標記級別的監督。在TOFU和RWKU中，ATWU實現了最先進的遺忘-保留權衡，超越了樣本級方法、基於概率的標記加權啟發式方法和基於輔助模型的方法。此外，學習到的分數與真實的遺忘特定範圍有著顯著更好的對齊，這表明ATWU識別了語義上有意義的標記級遺忘信號。總體而言，我們的結果表明，保留衝突提供了一個有效的標準，用於識別語言模型應該遺忘的內容，使得能夠直接從模型表示中無監督地學習標記級遺忘特異性，並且計算開銷最小。
2606.06320.author: Gizem Yüce et.al.
2606.06320.authors: Gizem Yüce, Giorgos Nikolaou, Nicolas Flammarion
